subject: Public Health
title: The Plague of Justinian
description: Learn The Plague of Justinian in one minute.

hook.text: In 541 CE, bubonic plague struck Constantinople—killing 5,000 people every single day at its peak.

buildup.text: The bacterium Yersinia pestis arrived on rat fleas aboard grain ships from Egypt.

discovery.text: Emperor Justinian himself caught the plague but survived. His dream of reuniting Rome did not.

twist.text: The plague returned in waves for 200 years, killing an estimated 25 to 50 million people.

climax.text: It reshaped Mediterranean civilization—depopulating cities and accelerating the fall of late antiquity.

punchline.text: An empire's ambition died on the back of a flea.

quiz.question: What caused the Plague of Justinian?
options: Yersinia pestis spread by rat fleas
